{{Infobox River | river_name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image_name = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | origin = [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] | basin_countries = [[Japan]] | length_km = 73.4 | elevation_m = 260 | mouth_elevation_m = 0 | discharge_m3/s = | watershed_km2 = 245.9 }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is {{km to mi|73.4}} in length and has a drainage area of {{km2 to mi2|260|precision=-1}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Isumi is designated as a Class 2 River. [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[ja:養老川]]

{{Infobox River | river_name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image_name = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | origin = [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] | basin_countries = [[Japan]] | length_km = 73.4 | elevation_m = 260 | mouth_elevation_m = 0 | discharge_m3/s = | watershed_km2 = 245.9 }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. == Sources == *{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) | title = Yōrō-gawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-03-01 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} == External Link == {{commonscat-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[ja:養老川]]

{{Infobox River | river_name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image_name = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | origin = [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] | basin_countries = [[Japan]] | length_km = 73.4 | elevation_m = 260 | mouth_elevation_m = 0 | discharge_m3/s = | watershed_km2 = 245.9 }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]]. {{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) | title = Yōrō-gawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-03-01 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[Keiyō Industrial Region]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Yōrōgawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-12-06 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Kominato Line]] of the [[Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[waterfalls]], and an [[onsen]] [[hot spring]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Link == {{commonscat-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[ja:養老川]]

Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[Keiyō Industrial Region]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Yōrōgawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-12-06 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Kominato Line]] of the [[Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[waterfalls]], and an [[onsen]] [[hot spring]]. {{Infobox River | river_name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image_name = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | origin = [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth = [[Tokyo Bay]] | basin_countries = [[Japan]] | length_km = 73.4 | elevation_m = 260 | mouth_elevation_m = 0 | discharge_m3/s = | watershed_km2 = 245.9 }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) | title = Yōrō-gawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-03-01 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[Keiyō Industrial Zone]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Yōrōgawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-12-06 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Kominato Line]] of the [[Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[waterfalls]], and an [[onsen]] [[hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichikawa, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commonscat-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]]

{{Infobox River | river_name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image_name = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | origin = [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth = [[Pacific Ocean]] | basin_countries = [[Japan]] | length_km = 73.4 | elevation_m = 260 | mouth_elevation_m = 0 | discharge_m3/s = | watershed_km2 = 245.9 }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[Chiba Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]]. {{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) | title = Yōrō-gawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-03-01 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[Keiyō Industrial Region]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Encyclopedia of Japan | title = Yōrōgawa | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2011-12-06 | year = 2011 | publisher = Netto Adobansusha | location = Tokyo}} The [[Kominato Line]] of the [[Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[waterfalls]], and an [[onsen]] [[hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichikawa, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Link == {{commonscat-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[ja:養老川]]

It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[:en:Rice|Rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[:en:Bōsō Hill Range|Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] {{Infobox river | name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | image_caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | source1_location = [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth_location = [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] | subdivision_type1 = Country | subdivision_name1 = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | length = {{convert|73.4|km|mi|abbr=on}} | source1_elevation = {{convert|260|m|ft|abbr=on}} | mouth_elevation = {{convert|0|m|ft|abbr=on}} | discharge1_avg = | basin_size = {{convert|245.9|km2|sqmi|abbr=on}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[:en:Rice|Rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[:en:Bōsō Hill Range|Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[:en:Keiyō Industrial Zone|Keiyō Industrial Zone]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[:en:Kominato Line|Kominato Line]] of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] {{Japan-river-stub}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yōrō River]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[:en:Rice|Rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[:en:Bōsō Hill Range|Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[:en:Keiyō Industrial Zone|Keiyō Industrial Zone]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[:en:Kominato Line|Kominato Line]] of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] {{Japan-river-stub}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yōrō River]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[:en:Rice|Rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[:en:Bōsō Hill Range|Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[:en:Keiyō Industrial Zone|Keiyō Industrial Zone]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[:en:Kominato Line|Kominato Line]] of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] {{Japan-river-stub}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Yōrō River]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] {{Infobox river | name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | image_caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | source1_location = [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]] and [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | mouth_location = [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] | subdivision_type1 = Country | subdivision_name1 = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | length = {{convert|73.4|km|mi|abbr=on}} | source1_elevation = {{convert|260|m|ft|abbr=on}} | mouth_elevation = {{convert|0|m|ft|abbr=on}} | discharge1_avg = | basin_size = {{convert|245.9|km2|sqmi|abbr=on}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of [[:en:Kamogawa, Chiba|Kamogawa]] and [[:en:Ōtaki, Chiba|Ōtaki]], and then passes through Ōtaki and [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for [[:en:Rice|Rice]] production. The river flows through significant parts of the [[:en:Bōsō Hill Range|Bōsō Hill Range]] before it empties into [[:en:Tokyo Bay|Tokyo Bay]] in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the [[:en:Keiyō Industrial Zone|Keiyō Industrial Zone]].{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[:en:Kominato Line|Kominato Line]] of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] [[:en:Hot spring|Hot spring]]. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} {{Japan-river-stub}} [[en:Yōrō River]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] {{Infobox river | name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | image_caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | source1_location = {{ill|Ōtaki, Chiba|en|Ōtaki, Chiba|WD=Q1346191|lt=Ōtaki}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} | mouth_location = {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|WD=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay}} | subdivision_type1 = Country | subdivision_name1 = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | length = {{convert|73.4|km|mi|abbr=on}} | source1_elevation = {{convert|260|m|ft|abbr=on}} | mouth_elevation = {{convert|0|m|ft|abbr=on}} | discharge1_avg = | basin_size = {{convert|245.9|km2|sqmi|abbr=on}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa}} and {{ill|Ōtaki, Chiba|en|Ōtaki, Chiba|WD=Q1346191|lt=Ōtaki}}, and then passes through Ōtaki and {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara}}.{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for {{ill|Rice|en|Rice|WD=Q5090|lt=Rice}} production. The river flows through significant parts of the {{ill|Bōsō Hill Range|en|Bōsō Hill Range|WD=Q5005697|lt=Bōsō Hill Range}} before it empties into {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|WD=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay}} in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the {{ill|Keiyō Industrial Zone|en|Keiyō Industrial Zone|WD=Q3276275|lt=Keiyō Industrial Zone}}.{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The {{ill|Kominato Line|en|Kominato Line|WD=Q6428347|lt=Kominato Line}} of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} {{ill|Hot spring|en|Hot spring|WD=Q177380|lt=Hot spring}}. [[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} {{Japan-river-stub}} [[en:Yōrō River]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[File:Yōrō Valley 01.jpg|thumb|View of Yōrō River from the Hōei Bridge, Ichihara, Chiba]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External links == {{commons category-inline|Yōrō River}} {{coord missing|Chiba Prefecture}} {{Rivers of Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{citation needed|Yoro River}} {{Japan-river-stub}} [[en:Yōrō River]] {{wikidata link|Q8062667}} [[Category:Rivers of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Japan]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[co:commons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yōrō River]][[en:Yōrō River]][[fa:رود یوری]][[ja:養老川]] {{Infobox river | name = Yōrō River
養老川 | image = Takataki-01.JPG | image_size = 300px | image_caption = Taka Falls, Yōrō River | source1_location = {{ill|Ōtaki, Chiba|en|Ōtaki, Chiba|WD=Q1346191|lt=Ōtaki|1=Ōtaki, Chiba}} and {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa|1=Kamogawa, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} | mouth_location = {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|WD=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}} | subdivision_type1 = Country | subdivision_name1 = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | length = {{convert|73.4|km|mi|abbr=on}} | source1_elevation = {{convert|260|m|ft|abbr=on}} | mouth_elevation = {{convert|0|m|ft|abbr=on}} | discharge1_avg = | basin_size = {{convert|245.9|km2|sqmi|abbr=on}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yōrō River'''|養老川|Yōrōgawa}} is a river in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is {{convert|73.4|km|mi|sp=us}} in length and has a drainage area of {{convert|260|km2|sqmi|sp=us}}. Under the Rivers Act of 1906 the Yōrō is designated as a Class 2 River. The Yōrō originates near [[Mount Kiyosumi]] on the border of Amatsukominato area of {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|en|Kamogawa, Chiba|WD=Q387211|lt=Kamogawa|1=Kamogawa, Chiba}} and {{ill|Ōtaki, Chiba|en|Ōtaki, Chiba|WD=Q1346191|lt=Ōtaki|1=Ōtaki, Chiba}}, and then passes through Ōtaki and {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|WD=Q207881|lt=Ichihara|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}.{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系 “Compendium of Japanese Historical Place Names”) |title=Yōrō-gawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-03-01 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The [[Yōrō Ravine]] and [[Awamata Falls]] are located in the upper part of the Yōrō, and it meets the [[Koshikiya River]], the [[Heizō River]], and the [[Uchida River]] at its midpoint. Land around the middle and lower parts of the Yōrō have been developed for {{ill|Rice|en|Rice|WD=Q5090|lt=Rice|1=Rice}} production. The river flows through significant parts of the {{ill|Bōsō Hill Range|en|Bōsō Hill Range|WD=Q5005697|lt=Bōsō Hill Range|1=Bōsō Hill Range}} before it empties into {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|WD=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}} in the Goi and Iwazaki districts of Ichihara. Land reclamation at the mouth of the Yōrō is a component of the {{ill|Keiyō Industrial Zone|en|Keiyō Industrial Zone|WD=Q3276275|lt=Keiyō Industrial Zone|1=Keiyō Industrial Zone}}.{{cite encyclopedia|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Japan |title=Yōrōgawa |url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |access-date=2011-12-06 |year=2011 |publisher=Netto Adobansusha |location=Tokyo |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ |archive-date=August 25, 2007 }} The {{ill|Kominato Line|en|Kominato Line|WD=Q6428347|lt=Kominato Line|1=Kominato Line}} of the [[:en:Kominato Railway|Kominato Railway]] roughly follows the path of the Yōrō and provides access to many of the tourist destinations along the river, including mountains, [[:en:Waterfalls|Waterfalls]], and an {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} {{ill|Hot spring|en|Hot spring|WD=Q177380|lt=Hot spring|1=Hot spring}}.
